title: Does every Paley graph have an internal partition?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would cleanly seal the short conference-graph theorem once the exact slice is frozen, but publication audit did not clear the folklore-risk gate. Formalizing now would be optional polish rather than the next seal.
micro_paper_assessment: Conditional pass: this is a real one-theorem conference-graph slice with a Paley corollary, but bounded publication audit did not clear folklore risk for the broader statement.
strongest_honest_claim: The packet appears to establish that in any conference graph G and any vertex v, the partition N[v] disjoint union (V(G) \ N[v]) is internal; hence every Paley graph P(q) admits an internal partition. This is stronger than an example, but bounded publication audit did not clear whether the conference-graph observation is already known in print.
paper_title_hint: Closed-Neighborhood Internal Partitions in Conference Graphs
candidate_theorem_slice: In any conference graph G on n vertices and any vertex v, the partition N[v] disjoint union (V(G) \ N[v]) is internal; therefore every Paley graph P(q) has an internal partition.
next_action: Do one last source-level prior-art check at the conference-graph / strongly-regular / Ban-Linial level. If it stays clear, freeze the conference-graph theorem slice and then Lean it. If a prior source appears, mark REDISCOVERY and retire without broadening scope.
